recalls.Description: Grizzly Industrial is recalling the G0815 15" 3HP heavy-duty planers due to potential impact and laceration hazards. These planers, designed for smoothing and flattening boards, measure 32 inches in width, 28 inches in depth, and 23.5 inches in height. The model number 'G0815' and the website 'grizzly.com' are printed on the front of the unit.
recalls.Title: Grizzly Industrial Recalls G0815 15" 3HP Heavy-Duty Planers Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Grizzly Industrial at 888-615-7944 anytime, email recalls@grizzly.com, or visit www.grizzly.com/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: G0815 15" 3HP Heavy-Duty Planer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,300 (In addition, 12 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Grizzly Industrial 15” 3HP Heavy-Duty Planer

Injuries.Name: Grizzly Industrial has received seven reports of the rotating cutters of the planers breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Bibb Tool & Cutter, Hamilton Tool and Supply, PMC Tool Company, Stoll Brothers Lumber, and various hardware stores nationwide, as well as online at Grizzly.com, HomeDepot.com, TractorSupply.com, MaxTool.com, and other websites from June 2016 through September 2025, priced between $900 and $2,400.

Importers.Name: Grizzly Industrial Inc., of Bellingham, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of impact and laceration due to chip breaker contacting cutterhead blades

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit with upgraded components and installation instructions

recalls.metaDescription: Grizzly Industrial recalls G0815 planers due to safety hazards. Contact for free repair kit and details.

recalls.Description: Milwaukee Tool has issued a recall for its M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaws, specifically catalog number 2826-20, which feature a serial number containing an 'A' in the fourth digit. This battery-operated chainsaw is available with either a 12" or 14" bar and is designed to work with Milwaukee's M18 batteries. The catalog number is located on the upper right side of the product's nameplate, while the serial number can be found on the upper left side. The chainsaw may be sold individually as a bare tool or as part of a kit that includes batteries and a charger.
recalls.Title: Milwaukee Tool Recalls M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaws Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Milwaukee Tool at 833-953-2012 from 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found on their eService portal at https://service.milwaukeetool.com/support/eservice or by visiting https://www.milwaukeetool.com/news/press-releases and selecting the 2025 tab. Consumers can also access recall details at http://www.milwaukeetool.com/support/recall.

Products.Name: Milwaukee Tool M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaw (12" or 14" bar)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 90,860 (In addition, about 7,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Milwaukee M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaw
Recalled Milwaukee M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaw (catalog/serial number label)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the chain brake not activating, including one injury involving a lacerated finger.

Manufacturers.Name: Milwaukee Electric Tool Corp., of Brookfield, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Available at Home Depot and various home improvement retailers, as well as online at homedepot.com, from March 2023 through September 2024, priced around $350 for the tool alone or $790 with batteries and charger.

Hazards.Name: Potential laceration risk due to chain brake failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the chainsaw immediately and contact Milwaukee Tool for a free repair. Register your product at https://service.milwaukeetool.com/support/eservice to obtain a prepaid shipping label for product return.

recalls.metaDescription: Milwaukee Tool recalls M18 FUEL Top Handle Chainsaws due to a laceration hazard. Learn how to get a free repair.

recalls.Description: Makita U.S.A., Inc. has announced a recall of its Professional Cordless Grease Guns and Grease Gun Hoses due to a potential laceration hazard. The affected grease guns include models XPG01S1, XPG01SR1, and XPG01Z, while the hoses involved are identified by model numbers 191A79-9, 191A80-4, 191W58-9, and 191W59-7. All recalled items feature spring ends, with model numbers located on the manufacturer label on the right side of the grease guns. Serial numbers for the affected grease guns can be found on the battery mounting port.
recalls.Title: Makita Recalls Cordless Grease Guns and Hoses Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Makita U.S.A., Inc. at 800-462-5482 from 8 a.m. to 7:45 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email greasegunrecall@makitausa.com. More details are available on their website at www.makitatools.com/recall or by visiting www.makitatools.com and selecting "Important Safety Notice/Recall" under the Support section.

Products.Name: Makita Professional Cordless Grease Gun Model XPG01S1

Images.Caption: Recalled Cordless Grease Gun and location of Serial No.
Recalled Grease Gun Hose (Models 191A79-9, 191A80-4, 191W58-9 and 191W59-7) with spring end shown
Replacement Hose (Model 1916V6-0)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of incidents worldwide of the grease gun hose failing, including five laceration injuries. All five incidents were reported outside the United States.

Retailers.Name: Various hardware and home improvement retailers nationwide and online from June 2020 through January 2025, priced between $60 and $390.

Importers.Name: Makita U.S.A. Inc., of La Mirada, California

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard from ejected grease due to hose damage

Remedies.Name: Free replacement hose for recalled grease guns and hoses

recalls.metaDescription: Makita recalls cordless grease guns and hoses due to laceration hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: TTI Consumer Power Tools has announced a recall for the RIDGID 18Volt Brushless 21-Degree and 30-Degree Framing Nailers due to a potential laceration hazard. The affected models can unintentionally discharge nails if the trigger is pulled without the necessary safety precautions being met. Consumers should check the data plate on the side of the nail tray or the bottom of the nailer for model and serial numbers to determine if their product is included in this recall.
recalls.Title: TTI Consumer Power Tools Issues Recall for RIDGID Framing Nailers Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact TTI Consumer Power Tools at 800-597-9624 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit powertools.ridgid.com/recall for further details and to check if your nailer is affected.

Products.Name: RIDGID 18Volt Brushless 21-Degree Framing Nailer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 64,000 (In addition, about 6,575 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled RIDGID 18Volt Brushless 21-Degree Framing Nailer
Recalled RIDGID 18Volt Brushless 30-Degree Framing Nailer

Retailers.Name: Available at Home Depot and Direct Tools Factory Outlet stores nationwide, as well as online at www.homedepot.com and www.directtoolsoutlet.com from July 2021 through May 2025, priced between $329 and $389.

Importers.Name: TTI Consumer Power Tools, Inc., of Anderson, South Carolina

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration due to malfunctioning dual action engagement system

Remedies.Name: Stop using the nailers and follow the instructions at powertools.ridgid.com/recall for a free repair.

recalls.metaDescription: TTI recalls RIDGID Framing Nailers due to a laceration hazard. Check your model and get a free repair today.

recalls.Description: Tractor Supply has announced a recall of several models of Traveller Aluminum Loading Ramps, including the 3,000 lb. 12" x 90" Arched Ramp, the 1,800 lb. 77" Straight Ramp, and the 1,500 lb. 12" x 84" Arched Ramp. These ramps are designed for loading various vehicles such as riding lawn mowers, ATVs, and golf carts onto trailers or pickup beds. However, they may not support the specified weight limits, leading to potential bending or breaking during use, which poses a serious fall hazard.
recalls.Title: Tractor Supply Issues Recall for Traveller Aluminum Loading Ramps Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled ramps immediately. For further assistance, contact Tractor Supply Company at 877-718-6750 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT Monday through Saturday, or from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. CT on Sunday. Additional information can be found online at https://www.tractorsupply.com/tsc/cms/policies-information/customer-solutions/recall-notices or by visiting www.tractorsupply.com and clicking on 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Traveller 3,000 lb. 12" x 90" Aluminum Sheet Arched Ramp
Recalled Traveller 1,800 lb. 77" Straight Ramp
Recalled Traveller 1,500 lb. 12" x 84" Aluminum Sheet Arched Ramp

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 35 reports of ramps bending or breaking, including two reports of ramps breaking and causing knee and other injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Tractor Supply Company locations across the country and online at www.tractorsupply.com from October 2024 through July 2025, priced between $180 and $220.

Importers.Name: Tractor Supply Company, of Brentwood, Tennessee

Hazards.Name: Inadequate weight support leading to bending or breaking, creating a fall risk.

Remedies.Name: Return the ramps to any Tractor Supply store for a full refund or send a photo of the product marked 'Recalled' to customersolutions@tractorsupply.com for further instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Tractor Supply recalls Traveller Aluminum Loading Ramps due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: DEWALT is recalling its Elite Grinder Series 4.5-inch 40G T27 High Density Flap Discs, specifically models DWA8280HRT and DWA8280RT. These discs, designed for heavy-duty angle grinders, may pose a risk as the ceramic cloth abrasive flaps can detach during use, leading to potential lacerations and injuries. Only products with date codes ranging from 2024-36 to 2024-48 are affected by this recall. The model number can be found on the front cover of the flap disc, while the date code is printed on the shipping boxes and stamped on the individual discs. If you're unsure about your product's date code, please reach out to DEWALT for assistance.
recalls.Title: DEWALT Recalls Elite Grinder Series Flap Discs Due to Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can visit DEWALT's website at https://www.dewalt.com/support/safety-notices-and-recalls/flap-disc-recall, call their toll-free number at 800-990-6421 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or email recall@sbdinc.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Model No. DWA8280HRT DEWALT Elite Series 40G T27 High Density Flap Disc
Recalled Model No. DWA8280RT DEWALT Elite Series 40G T27 High Density Flap Disc
Backside of recalled DEWALT Elite Series 40G T27 High Density Flap Disc
Label on the box for recalled DEWALT Elite Series 40G T27 High Density Flap Disc

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the flap disc coming apart while in use on the grinder, including three injuries resulting in cuts and scrapes.

Retailers.Name: Available at industrial supply stores nationwide, including Grainger, Fastenal, and Airgas, as well as online at Amazon.com from September 2024 through May 2025, priced between $7 and $10.

Importers.Name: DEWALT Industrial Tool Company, of Towson, Maryland

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration and injury due to detachment of abrasive flaps

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled flap discs immediately and visit the DEWALT website to participate in the recall. Consumers will need to write 'Recall' on the disc, upload a photo, and certify its disposal to receive a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall of DEWALT Elite Grinder Flap Discs due to injury risks. Check if your product is affected and learn how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: Masco Canada Limited and Delta Faucet Company have issued a recall for all Delta-branded Gas Ball Valves. These chrome, manually operated valves are designed for use in laboratories within schools, research facilities, and hospitals, functioning as on/off controls for gas flow. Each valve features the name 'Delta' on one side and 'GAS' in white on the blue top, with identifiers such as '1/2 PSI MAX' and 'GAS/GAZ' stamped on the bottom for easy recognition.
recalls.Title: Recall of Delta Gas Ball Valves Due to Fire and Burn Risks by Masco Canada
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Masco Canada by calling 888-789-6779 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, emailing recall@mascocanada.com, or visiting https://www.mascocanada.com/recall-notices. Additional information is available at www.mascocanada.com under the 'Recalls' section at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Delta Gas Ball Valves
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,700 (In addition, about 18,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve with “1/2 PSI MAX” and “GAS/GAZ” identifiers

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at plumbing supply distributors across the country from January 2006 to January 2025, priced between $30 and $160.

Importers.Name: Delta Faucet Company, of Indianapolis, Indiana

Hazards.Name: Potential gas leaks from the valve in the open position, leading to fire and burn risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled valves and contact Masco Canada to verify if their product is affected and to receive refund instructions. Masco Canada will also cover labor costs for the removal and disposal of the valves. The company is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Delta Gas Ball Valves recalled due to fire risks. Contact Masco Canada for refunds and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: Alliance4Safety, alongside 31 furniture manufacturers, is recalling plastic tip-over restraint kits used with clothing storage units due to potential safety hazards. These kits, which include a plastic zip-tie, two brackets, and two screws, were sold with furniture made in Vietnam and may pose a risk of tip-over and entrapment, particularly for children. The kits are packaged in white with black lettering and contain instructions for anchoring furniture. The manufacturing date is indicated on a sticker or stamp as November 2019 or later.
recalls.Title: Recall of New Age Furniture Tip-over Restraint Kits by Alliance4Safety and 31 Furniture Brands Due to Safety Risks for Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Alliance4Safety at 855-416-7370 at any time or visit www.alliance4safety.org/new-age-recall for more details.

Products.Name: New Age Furniture Tip-over Restraint Kits

Images.Caption: Recalled New Age tip restraint kit with plastic zip-tie, 2 brackets, and 2 screws
Packaging containing the recalled New Age tip restraint kits, stating "Manufactured by New Age Industries" at the bottom

Injuries.Name: Furniture companies have received two reports of the New Age tip kits breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: New Age Industries Co. LTD, of Vietnam

Retailers.Name: Available at furniture stores across the country and online from November 2019 to the present

Hazards.Name: Risk of tip-over and entrapment due to brittle plastic zip ties

Remedies.Name: Free replacement tip kit for affected units

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of New Age furniture tip-over restraint kits due to safety risks for children. Check if your kit is affected and get a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Ergodyne has issued a recall for the Squids 3705 Wire Tool Attachments, specifically the Screw Gate Tool Tail. This product is designed to securely tether tools, preventing them from falling while working at heights. The attachment features a 5 ¾-inch-long orange wire loop with a black and green tag, which displays 'Ergodyne' and 'Model 3705'. The back of the tag is green. This recall is limited to the model number 3705, which was sold in packs of six or as part of kits numbered 3170, 3183, 3188, and 3189.
recalls.Title: Ergodyne Recalls Squids 3705 Wire Tool Attachments Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled wire tool attachment immediately and reach out to Ergodyne at 800-522-0520 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. They can also email returns@ergodyne.com or visit https://www.ergodyne.com/product-recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Squids 3705 Wire Tool Attachments
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 16,570 (In addition, about 790 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Ergodyne Squids 3705 with Model Number

Retailers.Name: Available through industrial and safety product distributors nationwide and online at https://www.ergodyne.com/ and other platforms like Amazon.com from September 2018 to July 2023, priced around $20 for a six-pack or up to $400 as part of kits.

Importers.Name: Tenacious Holdings Inc., dba Ergodyne, of St. Paul, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to potential breakage of the wire tool attachment when tethered to tools over two pounds.

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a prepaid shipping label to return the product and obtain a $4 refund per wire tool (up to $24 for six-pack) as credit towards another Ergodyne product.

recalls.metaDescription: Ergodyne recalls Squids 3705 Wire Tool Attachments due to injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact Ergodyne for a refund.
